flashcards.definition: Computer models that solve atmospheric physics equations using current observations to forecast future weather.
Instrument package carried by weather balloon that measures temperature, humidity, pressure, and wind through the atmosphere.
Radar that measures precipitation intensity AND wind velocity/direction by detecting the Doppler shift of returned signals.
Running multiple model simulations with slightly varied initial conditions to assess forecast uncertainty and probability.
Sensitivity to initial conditions means small errors grow over time, limiting weather forecast reliability beyond ~10 days.
